title: 2.2 Flowering and Non-flowering Plants
description: Flowering and non-flowering plants — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: With what features is the concept of a plant widely associated?
options: Flowers, root, stem and leaves having transporting vessels
questions.correctAnswer: Flowers, root, stem and leaves having transporting vessels

questions.question: What are mosses and liverworts classified as?
What do non-vascular plants lack?
options: A transporting system or conducting vessels
questions.correctAnswer: A transporting system or conducting vessels

questions.question: What is a characteristic of non-vascular plants regarding their size?
options: They are generally small with limited height
They can grow to any height
questions.correctAnswer: They are generally small with limited height

questions.question: Where are non-vascular plants restricted to?
Do lower plants produce seeds, flowers and fruits?
options: They are seedless and do not have flowers and fruits
questions.correctAnswer: They are seedless and do not have flowers and fruits

questions.question: What structure serves as the reproductive organ in gymnosperms instead of flowers?
How are seeds in gymnosperm cones described?
What are flowering plants commonly known as?
What feature distinguishes angiosperms from gymnosperms?
options: Angiosperms have flowers and produce seeds within a fruit
questions.correctAnswer: Angiosperms have flowers and produce seeds within a fruit

questions.question: What are gymnosperm seeds produced in their cone described as?
